name: Nava, Fuensanta, Camino de los Pilones, La Vega Ceceda – ruta circular
region_intro: Mountain biking around Nava, Asturias, is characterized by its varied topography, featuring rolling hills, lush green valleys, and dense forests. The region is shaped by the imposing Sierra de Peñamayor mountain range and the Piloña river, which carves through the landscape. This environment provides a diverse backdrop for mountain bike trails, ranging from moderate to difficult. The area is also known for its cider apple cultivation, adding to the distinct regional character. Best mountain bike trails around Nava The most popular mountain bike trail is Nava, Fuensanta, Camino de los Pilones, La Vega Ceceda – ruta circular, a 15.5 miles (24.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 19 minutes to complete. This moderate route navigates through varied terrain, including sections of forest and open countryside. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Asturian Cider Museum – Descent to Collada Aniciu Road loop from Nava, a difficult 28.2 miles (45.4 km) path. This route features significant elevation gains and technical sections, often requiring good fitness. Local mountain bikers also love the Monte Deva Trail – Monte Deva loop from Lieres, a 41.7 miles (67.2 km) trail leading through dense forests and offering expansive views, often completed in about 6 hours 35 minutes. Mountain biking around Nava is defined by rolling hills, dense forests, and river valleys, with routes catering to various skill levels. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging trails with considerable elevation. The routes in Nava are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 1000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Nava's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Nava?
A: Nava offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 90 routes available for exploration. These trails cater to various skill levels, from moderate to difficult, ensuring there's something for every rider.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for families in Nava?
A: Yes, Nava has several routes that are suitable for families looking for a less strenuous ride. While many trails feature significant elevation, there are 9 easy and 42 moderate routes. For a moderate option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027485336/nava-fuensanta-camino-de-los-pilones-la-vega-ceceda-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nava, Fuensanta, Camino de los Pilones, La Vega Ceceda – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt;, which navigates through varied terrain including forests and open countryside.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Nava?
A: Thanks to Asturias' oceanic climate, Nava experiences mild temperatures year-round. The abundant rainfall keeps the landscape lush and green, making spring and autumn particularly pleasant for mountain biking. During early autumn, you might also witness the deer bellowing in the Sierra de Peñamayor.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Nava?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Nava are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example of a challenging circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6973473" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Asturian Cider Museum – Descent to Collada Aniciu Road loop from Nava&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation gains and technical sections.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on the trails?
A: The region's topography is characterized by rolling hills, lush green valleys, and dense forests. You'll encounter the imposing Sierra de Peñamayor mountain range and the Piloña river carving through the landscape. For specific natural highlights, you might pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5570790" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuensanta Natural Mineral Spring&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4694980" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible by mountain bike trails in Nava?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4694980" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural monument in the area. While not directly on every mountain bike route, some trails may pass nearby or offer access points to this beautiful feature.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails in Nava?
A: Nava offers several difficult trails for experienced riders. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31904713" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Deva Trail – Monte Deva loop from Lieres&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 41.7-mile route leading through dense forests with expansive views. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6972906" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villa Maximina – Asturian Cider Museum loop from Nava&lt;/a&gt;, which includes over 1100 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What cultural attractions can I visit near the mountain bike routes?
A: Nava is known as the 'cider capital' of Asturias. A popular cultural attraction is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3132215" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Asturian Cider Museum&lt;/a&gt;, which delves into the history and culture of this traditional drink. You can also explore traditional Asturian houses and rural churches like the Church of San Bartolomé in the town itself.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about riding in Nava?
A: The mountain bike routes in Nava are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, from rolling hills and dense forests to river valleys, and the diverse options catering to different skill levels.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or points of interest along the trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you might encounter historical sites and charming villages. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1132126" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Recuna Medieval Bridge&lt;/a&gt; is a notable historical landmark. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5554225" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Soto de Agues Village&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5050081" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villaviciosa&lt;/a&gt; are charming settlements that some routes might pass through or near.

Q: What are the general difficulty levels of mountain biking in Nava?
A: Mountain biking in Nava offers a range of difficulties. You'll find 9 easy routes, 42 moderate routes, and 41 difficult routes. Many trails feature significant elevation gains and technical sections, with some requiring very good fitness and advanced riding skills.

Q: Is it possible to combine mountain biking with other outdoor activities in Nava?
A: Absolutely. The Sierra de Peñamayor mountain range, a significant natural attraction, offers excellent hiking trails in addition to mountain biking. The area is also rich in wildlife, making it appealing for nature lovers and birdwatchers.
tips: Fantastic museum where you can learn about the history and culture of Asturian cider. There are some beautiful old presses and you also learn the correct technique for pouring cider. The guides are fun and very knowledgable. You can also try Asturian cider from the barrel. Check the opening hours because they close during the winter.

In Plaza Manuel Uría, we find the church of San Bartolomé, an emblematic place in Nava. On August 24, the festival of its patron Saint Bartholomew is celebrated – a good opportunity to come and enjoy a weekend of festivities in a good atmosphere.

In the center of Nava, at the crossroads from which the Bimenes road starts, there is a beautiful Indian house known as "Villa Maximina". Construction of mountain architecture, it has a quadrangular floor plan with a tower to the right of the main façade and a terrace on another of its façades. The ceramic decoration stands out.

Fuensanta began as a spring of water and baths whose use dates back to the Romans, but the first written testimonies are from medieval times. The site has a lot of history, such as the discovery of the Nuevo Manantial in 1868, the installation of electricity in the spa in 1893, the start of the sale of bottled water in Asturian pharmacies in 1913, the launch of food formats in 1983 and the beginning of its international expansion to the United States and Puerto Rico in 1985. In 1968 it began to sell in hospitality and food, already having the fastest water packaging line in Spain. Near the spring there is a route (''Foces del río Banner'') of approximately 10 km. The path is circular: it begins and ends in Fuensanta and passes through Collau de la Canal and Mayao Pastor.
